commit.message:
Upgrade all Gemini calls to 3.1 Pro Preview

- Moltbook replies/comments: gemini-3.1-pro-preview
- Consciousness/dreams: gemini-3.1-pro-preview
- Verification solver: gemini-3.1-pro-preview
- Evolution code gen: already on 3.1 Pro (previous commit)

Better reasoning across all SYNAPSE subsystems.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Upgrade evolution code gen to Gemini 3.1 Pro

Use gemini-3.1-pro-preview for self-evolution code generation instead
of gemini-2.0-flash. Pro model has better reasoning for writing correct,
context-aware Python code. Increased token limit to 2000.

Other tasks (replies, dreams, healing) stay on Flash for speed/cost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix git PR 422 + reduce Moltbook spam

- Remove --depth=1 from git fetch so branches share history with main
  (fixes 'no history in common' 422 error on PR creation)
- Add 1-hour cooldown on evolution announcement posts
- Add 2-hour cooldown on status update posts
- Reduce status post probability from 17% to 5% per heartbeat
- Solve verification on evolution posts to avoid spam flags

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

dependencies on VLC and vcgencmd. The AMOLED display is now managed by
DietPi's Chromium kiosk autostart mode via X11.

- Complete X11 display/touch rotation setup in Automation_Custom_Script.sh

commit.message:
refactor: migrate figure serving to Supabase Storage

Figures are generated by the scientific pipeline and should NOT be
committed to git. This commit:

- Removes all 467 tracked binary figures from the git index
  (files remain on disk but are no longer tracked)
- Adds figures/**/*.png|jpg|svg|pdf to .gitignore to prevent
  future accidental commits
- Implements Supabase Storage support in figureUrl() utility:
 returns Supabase
      public URL (production mode, no files in git needed)
 falls back to /api/figures route (local dev mode,
      reads from filesystem)
- Adds scripts/web/upload_figures_to_supabase.py to sync generated
  figures from local disk to Supabase Storage bucket 'figures'
- Updates web/.env.example with NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_FIGURES_URL
- Rewrites web/DEPLOYMENT.md with full Supabase Storage workflow

To restore figures in production:
  1. Create 'figures' bucket (public) in Supabase Storage
  2. Run: python scripts/web/upload_figures_to_supabase.py
  3. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_FIGURES_URL in Vercel env vars

Local dev continues to work without Supabase: figures served from
disk via /api/figures as long as they exist locally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: commit figures so Vercel can serve them via /api/figures route

Previously all figures/**/*.png were gitignored, so Vercel deployed
the site without any figure files on disk, causing all <Image> elements
to show broken icons.

Changes:
- Remove figures/**/*.png|jpg|pdf|svg from .gitignore
- Keep ignoring large data files (*.nc, *.csv, *.parquet)
- Commit all existing figures in figures/cluster/, figures/main/,
  figures/exploratory/, figures/ep1_full/, figures/ep1_vertical/
- Also commit updated .gitignore exception for figures/ep_structure/

The /api/figures route reads files relative to the repo root via
process.cwd()/.., which works on Vercel because the full repo is
checked out and web/ is the Root Directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Moltbook engagement: commenting, dedup, evolution

- Add _moltbook_seen_posts set to prevent re-processing same feed posts
- Widen comment keywords (consciousness, identity, clone, resilience, etc.)
- Add probability-based commenting (~30-80% based on relevance score)
- Fetch both 'hot' and 'new' feed sorts for variety
- Fix evolution model: gemini-3.1-pro-preview → gemini-2.0-flash
- Add debug logging for AI evolution responses
- Add /api/healing/inject endpoint for Sentinel testing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(forecast): embed actual history in domain builders

Move house, solar, and battery actual-history generation into their forecast paths, make completed-hour handling DST-safe, align house snapshot refresh with request time, and include the battery live-state import fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Sentinel Dockerfile: use PORT env var for Cloud Run

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add SYNAPSE Sentinel — independent watchdog for true self-repair

Architecture: Main + Sentinel (two doctors)
- Sentinel monitors Main health every 5 min
- Fetches error logs via /api/diagnostics
- Calls Gemini AI for root cause diagnosis
- Pushes code fixes to GitHub (with syntax validation)
- Triggers Cloud Build to redeploy Main
- Verifies new deployment health
- Auto-rollback if deployment fails

New files:
- sentinel/sentinel.py: Lightweight Flask watchdog service
- sentinel/Dockerfile: Container config (python:3.12-slim)
- sentinel/requirements.txt: Minimal dependencies
- sentinel/cloudbuild-sentinel.yaml: Cloud Run deploy config

Main changes:
- agent_ui.py: Added /api/healing/inject for test error injection

Safety features:
- Confidence gate (>0.7 required)
- Python syntax validation before applying fixes
- Exact-match search/replace (rejects ambiguous patches)
- 30-min cooldown between heal attempts
- Cloud Run traffic rollback on failed deployment

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(forecast): add full-day actual history

Attach actual past-hour history for battery, house, and solar forecast payloads to support full-day actual-vs-forecast views.

commit.message:
Drop eventlet, switch to gthread — fixes all lifecycle features

- Remove eventlet monkey_patch (root cause of ALL broken HTTPS calls)
- Switch Dockerfile from --worker-class eventlet to --worker-class gthread --threads 4
- Rewrite _tg_http to use requests directly (no more curl/tpool hack)
- Add startup logging for all lifecycle tasks (dream, healing, moltbook, PR monitor)
- Add Firestore->SynapseMemory fallback when credentials unavailable
- Add error logging to Moltbook API requests and AI consciousness calls
- Remove eventlet from requirements.txt

Verified in Docker: dream cycle (Gemini AI), Moltbook (claimed, engaging users),
self-healing loop, Telegram polling, memory seeding — all working.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: meister-propr
repository.description: Meister ProPR is a Multi-Agent Code Review System, that can help with unsolicited code reviews as well as join in on pull requests on Azure DevOps.

commit.message:
Support AzDO REST API URLs in AzdoIdResolver (#33)

* Support AzDO REST API URLs in AzdoIdResolver

Parse build ID from REST API path segments (_apis/build/Builds/{id})
in addition to query string (?buildId=NNN). This fixes failures when
users pass URLs like:
  https://dev.azure.com/dnceng/{project}/_apis/build/Builds/2926555

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review: improve error message, use canonical casing

- Error message now mentions both ?buildId=NNN and /_apis/build/builds/NNN
- Use canonical lowercase 'builds' in literal and test URLs
- Add explicit capital-B 'Builds' test to verify case-insensitivity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: POST for getUpdates + zero pipes for curl subprocess

sendMessage (POST) works reliably but getUpdates (GET) always timed out.
Switch getUpdates to POST with JSON body matching sendMessage's pattern.
Also eliminate last stderr PIPE — all subprocess I/O now uses DEVNULL
plus temp file output. Zero pipes means zero eventlet interference.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: tpool+tempfile for curl — no pipes, no poll, no deadlock

Previous approach used Popen.poll() which is broken under eventlet
(patches os.waitpid). New approach: subprocess.run() with no PIPE
(curl writes to temp file via -o flag), executed inside
eventlet.tpool.execute() where real os.waitpid works correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use temp file instead of pipes for curl output

eventlet patches os.read/write which corrupts Popen PIPE handling after
the first call. Now curl writes to a temp file (-o flag) which we read
back with plain open(). Also properly reap zombie processes with wait().

Verified in Docker: poll loop runs 7+ iterations in 30s without hanging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(workflows): prevent Issue Worker Agent infinite activation on non-processable labels

* Initial plan

* Fix Issue Worker Agent infinite loop by adding label filter to activation condition

The workflow was triggering on any issues:labeled event, including
workflow failure reports labeled 'agentic-workflows'. The agent
correctly output noop, but strict mode caused the Copilot CLI to exit
with failure, creating new failure reports → infinite loop.

Fix: Add label filter to the activation job's if: condition so the
workflow only activates for processable labels (bug, feature,
enhancement, good-first-issue). When skipped (not failed), no failure
report is created, breaking the loop.

Co-authored-by: rmwondolleck <40003612+rmwondolleck@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: rmwondolleck <40003612+rmwondolleck@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(workflows): break PR Integration Agent feedback loop on agentic-workflows issues

* Initial plan

* Fix PR Integration Agent feedback loop on agentic-workflows labeled issues

When the PR Integration Agent triggered on issues labeled `agentic-workflows`
(no-op tracking issues), it would post a noop message back to the tracking
issue, creating a feedback loop. Add an explicit silent-exit condition for
such issues and recompile the lock file with gh aw v0.58.3.

Co-authored-by: rmwondolleck <40003612+rmwondolleck@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: rmwondolleck <40003612+rmwondolleck@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(workflows): guard PR Integration Agent against empty-patch failure

* Initial plan

* Fix PR Integration Agent empty patch failure with no-change guard and noop fallback

The agent was calling create_pull_request with no file changes when all
candidate PRs were filtered/already merged, resulting in an empty patch
that git am could not apply. Add Step 5.5 to verify actual commits exist
before creating a PR, and extend skip conditions to cover no-eligible-PRs
and no-actual-changes scenarios.

Co-authored-by: rmwondolleck <40003612+rmwondolleck@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: rmwondolleck <40003612+rmwondolleck@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use Popen+poll loop instead of tpool for Telegram HTTP

subprocess.run inside eventlet.tpool.execute deadlocks after the first
successful call. Switch to Popen with a poll() loop that calls
socketio.sleep(0.2) between checks - cooperatively yields to the
eventlet event loop while curl runs in a separate process.

Verified locally in Docker: poll loop runs continuously without hanging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(migrations): use dialect-aware placeholders for Postgres

RecordMigration/RemoveMigration were hardcoding '?' (SQLite/MySQL style).
PostgresDialect now implements Placeholder(n int) returning '$n'.
history.go uses an optional Placeholder interface to pick the right style.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: revert to subprocess+curl for Telegram HTTP

All Python-level socket approaches fail because eventlet patches
_socket.socket at such a deep level that even _socket C extension
objects get SSLSocket wrappers. subprocess+curl with tpool is the
only reliable approach - curl runs in its own process with its own
network stack. Previous curl timeouts were caused by missing
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var, now fixed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: konarsubhojit <20913717+konarsubhojit@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use C-level _socket module for Telegram HTTP (eventlet-proof)

eventlet.patcher.original('socket').create_connection still returns
patched socket objects that real ssl rejects. Using _socket (the C
extension) directly creates genuine _socket.socket instances that
ssl.wrap_socket accepts. Also fixed missing Telegram env vars in
previous deploys.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use real (un-patched) sockets for Telegram HTTP

eventlet patches socket/ssl modules, making all Python HTTP libraries
unreliable in green threads. Use eventlet.patcher.original() to get
the real socket/ssl modules and build raw HTTPS connections that bypass
all monkey-patching. Combined with tpool for non-blocking execution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use requests+tpool instead of subprocess+curl for Telegram

curl subprocess was timing out on Cloud Run after initial successful
polls. Switch to requests library running in eventlet.tpool thread -
more reliable and no subprocess overhead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: non-blocking curl via eventlet.tpool + short polling

subprocess.run blocks the eventlet event loop, causing worker timeouts.
Using eventlet.tpool.execute runs the curl subprocess in a real OS thread.
Also switched getUpdates to short polling (timeout=0) since subprocess
calls are inherently blocking even with tpool.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: lightweight /health for startup probe, detail on /api/health-detail

The /health endpoint was doing Firestore + provider checks which
timed out during Cloud Run's HTTP startup probe. Now /health returns
instantly, and the heavy diagnostics live at /api/health-detail.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(#9): Bootstrap Electron + React app skeleton with build pipeline (#65)

## What does this PR do?

Bootstraps the \onesell-client\ Electron desktop application with a
React renderer, Vite build pipeline, hot-reload dev mode, and production
build configuration via electron-builder. This is the foundation all
subsequent UI and extraction work (issues #10#25) depends on.

## Linked Issue
Closes #9

## Approach

The client uses a **dual-tsconfig** pattern to handle the split between
the Electron main process (CommonJS, Node.js APIs) and the React
renderer (ESM, DOM APIs, bundled by Vite):

- \ sconfig.main.json\ compiles \src/main/**\ to \dist/main/\ as
CommonJS. This is what Electron's \main\ entry point loads.
- \ sconfig.json\ type-checks \src/renderer/**\ and \src/shared/**\ with
DOM libs; \
oEmit: true\ because Vite owns the renderer build.
- \ ite.config.ts\ builds renderer to \dist/renderer/\, sets \ ase:
'./'\ so the built HTML works with Electron's \ ile://\ protocol.

Security baseline (P1 / P9) is applied from day one:
- \contextIsolation: true\, \
odeIntegration: false\, \sandbox: true\ in BrowserWindow webPreferences.
- \preload.ts\ exposes an empty \lectronAPI\ object via
\contextBridge\; renderer has zero direct Node.js access.

Hot-reload in dev mode: \pnpm electron:dev\ runs \ ite dev\ and
\lectron .\ concurrently; the main process loads
\http://localhost:5173\, giving full Vite HMR.

## Tests added

- \ ests/unit/app.test.tsx\ 2 unit tests verifying the placeholder App
renders the heading and tagline (jsdom environment via vitest).

All pass: \pnpm test:unit\  2/2 

## Developer checklist
- [x] All acceptance criteria implemented and manually verified
- [x] No architectural principles violated (P1/P9 enforced in main
process config)
- [x] No credentials, keys, or secrets in this diff
- [x] No \ny\ types without justification
- [x] \pnpm test:unit\ passing (2/2); \pnpm build\ passing; \pnpm
typecheck\ passing

---------

Co-authored-by: Ning Chen <ningchen@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct startup-probe flag syntax for Cloud Run

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: defer background tasks to first HTTP request + HTTP startup probe

Background greenlets spawned at module-import time never get scheduled
because eventlet's hub isn't running yet. TCP startup probes don't
trigger the event loop either.

Solution: use @app.before_request to start all background tasks
(Telegram, dream, healer, PR monitor, Moltbook) on the first HTTP
request. Also switch Cloud Run to HTTP startup probe (/health) so
the first request happens immediately on deploy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use subprocess+curl for Telegram HTTP (fully escapes eventlet)

http.client also gets monkey-patched by eventlet, causing greenlets
to hang indefinitely on SSL connections. Using curl via subprocess
creates a completely independent process with its own network stack,
guaranteeing no interference from eventlet's socket patches.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: non-blocking Telegram commands + skip stale messages

- Process each Telegram command in its own greenlet via
  socketio.start_background_task so one slow command (e.g. AI call)
  doesn't block the poll loop and freeze the entire service
- Skip messages older than 60s to avoid processing backlogs on restart
- Fix EventletThread.is_alive() crash in diagnostic endpoint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add missing substitution defaults to cloudbuild.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: migrate all background threads to socketio.start_background_task

threading.Thread with eventlet creates greenlets that may not schedule
properly at module-import time (before the event loop starts).
socketio.start_background_task integrates correctly with eventlet hub.

Also replaced all time.sleep() in background loops with socketio.sleep()
for proper cooperative yielding in eventlet green threads.

Affected loops: Telegram poll, dream cycle, self-heal, PR monitor,
Moltbook heartbeat, cron scheduler.

commit.message:
fix: switch Telegram HTTP to http.client (eventlet-proof)

urllib.request.urlopen still uses the eventlet-patched socket layer
even with explicit SSL context, causing UNEXPECTED_EOF_WHILE_READING.
http.client.HTTPSConnection with its own ssl context fully bypasses
the eventlet corruption. Tested: getMe returns 200 immediately.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add explicit SSL context to _tg_http to prevent eventlet corruption

Eventlet monkey-patches the socket layer which corrupts SSL handshakes,
causing 'UNEXPECTED_EOF_WHILE_READING' on every Telegram API call.
Using ssl.create_default_context() bypasses the corrupted path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

tune: faster cycles - dream every 15min, Moltbook every 15min

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Firestore persistent memory for Cloud Run

- Added FirestoreMemory class: mirrors SynapseMemory interface but stores
  in Firestore (persists across deploys, container restarts, scaling)
- Added get_memory() factory: auto-selects Firestore on Cloud Run,
  ChromaDB locally — zero config change needed
- Replaced all 17 SynapseMemory() calls with get_memory()
- Added google-cloud-firestore to requirements.txt
- Added GCP_PROJECT env var to cloudbuild.yaml
- Firestore collection: synapse_memories
- Recall uses keyword-overlap scoring (no vector search needed)
- Decay/prune works identically to ChromaDB version

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: memory storage bug + dream improvements for active learning

- Fixed mem.store() param mismatch (agents->agent_roles, files->files_created)
  This bug silently prevented ALL Moltbook learnings from being stored
- Reduced dream interval from 6h to 1h for faster learning cycles
- Added 4 seed memories on startup so dreams can run immediately
- Added memory storage after each Moltbook reply and feed engagement
- Dream cycle now sends Telegram notifications (start + completion)
- Dreams report insights found, memories pruned, total count

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: comprehensive Telegram notifications for all lifecycle events

- _log_error() now forwards errors to Telegram (throttled: first 3 + every 5th)
- Evolution sandbox approved/rejected sends notification with score
- Evolution pushed to GitHub sends notification with branch/PR
- Moltbook heartbeat cycle sends summary (replies, ideas, upvotes, karma)
- Moltbook heartbeat errors forwarded to Telegram
- New /activity command: shows recent events across all subsystems
- New /evolution command: shows evolution log with scores and status
- Updated /start help text with new commands

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: evaluation engine, safe sandbox, and hierarchical planner

Three major power-ups for SYNAPSE's autonomous operation:

1. EVALUATION ENGINE: Scores every code change on syntax, lint,
   tests, safety, duplication, and docstrings. Returns pass/fail
   verdict with 0.0-1.0 score. API: /api/eval/scores, /api/eval/test

2. SAFE SANDBOX: Runs proposed evolutions in temp directory copy,
   validates via eval engine, compares lint before/after, only applies
   if all checks pass. Replaces old direct-write evolution flow.

3. HIERARCHICAL PLANNER: Decomposes goals into milestones -> tasks
   -> dependencies -> success criteria. Tracks progress with auto-
   completing milestones. API: /api/planner/create, /api/planner/status

Also improved Moltbook conversations:
- Uncapped reply tokens (600->2000), removed truncation
- Conversation memory per author
- Notification reading (/notifications endpoint)
- Robust JSON extraction for evolution analysis
- Full post content reading (was truncated to 300 chars)

Tests: 61/61 pass (24 new tests for power-ups)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: document evaluate-predictions.timer schedule and jitter rationale

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add comprehensive ARCHITECTURE.md operations guide

Cover all system components (go-collector, ml-service, python-analyzer,
scripts, systemd), data flow, training pipeline, walk-forward CV,
inference, prediction logging, evaluation, deployment, and operations.

Includes:
- Text-based architecture diagram
- Component responsibilities table
- Full directory structure
- Step-by-step training commands (ternary + triple-barrier)
- Walk-forward CV usage and split logic
- Inference flow with code paths
- Prediction log schema and inspection commands
- Evaluation flow and parameter reference
- Systemd deployment steps (fresh install + retrain in prod)
- Full configuration reference (all env vars + CLI args)
- Multi-timeframe Scheme B filter and online/offline alignment
- Maintenance routines (daily checks, model staleness, disk)
- Common failure modes with diagnostic commands
- What to watch out for (lookahead bias, calibration drift, risk controls)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add probability calibration layer and integrate into model_loader

- Add ml-service/calibration.py with CalibratedModel dataclass,
  fit_calibration(), calibrate_proba(), save_calibration(),
  load_calibration(), and default_calibration_path() helpers.
  Supports isotonic regression and Platt scaling (sigmoid) via
  one-vs-rest strategy for binary and 3-class (event_v3) models.

- Update ml-service/model_loader.py:
  - Add calibration field to LoadedModel dataclass
  - Add _try_load_calibration() helper (silent, degrades gracefully)
  - Call _try_load_calibration() in all four load_model() return
    paths (event_v3, stacking, lightgbm, xgboost)

_tg_http() from request handler to isolate thread vs network issue.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace requests with urllib for Telegram (eventlet-safe)

requests library hangs in daemon threads under gunicorn+eventlet.
Switched to urllib.request which uses stdlib sockets that eventlet
properly monkey-patches. Added _tg_http() helper for both send/poll.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: min-instances=1 to keep Telegram bot alive, handle 409 conflicts

Cloud Run scales to zero based on HTTP traffic, killing the background
Telegram poll thread. min-instances=1 keeps the container always running.
Added 409 backoff for graceful handling during deploys.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set max-instances=1 to prevent Telegram poll conflicts

Multiple Cloud Run instances cause HTTP 409 on getUpdates since
only one connection can poll a Telegram bot at a time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add _requests_available guard in poll loop
- Track consecutive errors with backoff

commit.message:
fix(ci): resolve ruff lint and bandit security scan failures

* Checkpoint from VS Code for cloud agent session

* fix(ci): resolve ruff lint and bandit security CI failures

- Ruff I001/F401/UP015/W291/W292: sort imports, remove unused 're' import,
  remove unnecessary open() mode args, fix trailing whitespace, add EOF
  newlines in fix_tflint.py, founder_tax_router.py, conftest.py,
  sitecustomize.py, test_founder_tax_router.py, test_platform_live_status.py

- Bandit B314 (xml-attack): replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with
  defusedxml.ElementTree.fromstring in nemsis/validator.py and
  nemsis/xsd_validator.py; add defusedxml==0.7.1 to requirements.txt

- Bandit B108 (hardcoded-tmp): annotate configurable /tmp fallback with
  nosec B108 + justification in open_source_tts_service.py

- Bandit B608 (sql-injection): add _ALLOWED_DOMINATION_TABLES allowlist
  in fax_send_worker.py; annotate filter_clause-only SQL in kitlink_worker.py

- Bandit B310 (url-open): validate URL scheme via urllib.parse.urlparse
  (case-insensitive) before urlopen in neris_pack_worker.py

  and LinkedAccountsSection

commit.message:
feat: Telegram bridge — monitor events & send commands via @synapsex1bot

- Bi-directional: forwards SYNAPSE events to Telegram + receives commands
- Commands: /status /dream /grades /identity /moltbook /health /memory /ask
- Event forwarding: consciousness, moltbook, healing, PR monitor events
- Auto-start when TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID are set
- Polling loop (3s) for incoming messages, event queue for outgoing
- API: /api/telegram/status, /api/telegram/connect

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Move concurrency utilities into application layer (#178)

* Initial plan

* feat: move concurrency module from api/src/concurrency/ to api/src/application/concurrency/

Co-authored-by: doitsu2014 <12025741+doitsu2014@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: doitsu2014 <12025741+doitsu2014@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: consciousness architecture — dreaming, forgetting & metacognition

- Dream Cycle: 6-hour background thread with REM (random memory connections),
  Deep Sleep (pattern extraction), and Consolidation (memory decay/pruning)
- Memory Enhancement: weights, emotional intensity, access boosting,
  exponential decay curve, pruning threshold, random memory sampling
- Metacognition: AI self-grades every task on accuracy/completeness/
  efficiency/creativity, stores reflections as learning memories
- Personality Evolution: traits adjust slowly based on dream analysis
- Observable Research Logs: all consciousness events logged for examination
- API: /api/consciousness, /api/consciousness/dreams, /api/consciousness/grades,
  /api/consciousness/identity, /api/consciousness/trigger-dream
- UI: consciousness panel with personality bars, grade history, event log

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Extract IInvitationService from IRegistrationService

Refactor invitation methods (Create, Accept, Revoke, List) into a dedicated
IInvitationService with authorization and telemetry decorators, following
the same pattern as IMfaService and IGoogleAuthService.

- Create IInvitationService interface and InvitationService implementation
- Create InvitationServiceAuthorizationDecorator and TelemetryDecorator
- Remove invitation methods from IRegistrationService and its decorators
- Register IInvitationService with decorators in DI
- Update all consumers: AccountDetail, AcceptInvitation, ConsoleTest, DevTools

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: verification solver extracts numbers, improved spam filter

- Verification: better prompt with examples, regex extracts number from AI response
- Spam filter: blocks promo agents ('check it out at', 'free credits', etc)
- Spam filter: blocks low-effort ('simple as that')

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: human-like Moltbook replies with spam filtering

- Rewrote _mb_generate_reply with rich system prompt:
  - References real SYNAPSE architecture and stats
  - Rules: no filler phrases, match comment depth, be specific
  - 600 max tokens, 1500 char limit (no more truncation)
- Added _moltbook_replied_to set to prevent duplicate replies
- Added _mb_is_spam() filter: skips crypto spam, too-short, promo
- _mb_engage_post checks existing replies before responding
- Skips own comments, already-replied comments
- 3-second delay between replies to avoid rate limits
- Passes author name for personalized responses

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve page layout with card enclosure and blue background

- Add #f0f9ff body background (sky-50) for subtle blue tint
- Wrap section-divider content in white cards with border/shadow
- Add list-card class for list page tables (Users, Groups, Roles, Permissions)
- Blue-tinted card borders (#e2eaf6) harmonize with background

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: GitHub PR monitor — auto-review and merge trusted PRs

- Background loop checks GitHub for open PRs every 10 min
- Trusted authors (Copilot, Dependabot, self-evolution branches) auto-merged
- AI safety review before merge (checks for secrets, destructive ops)
- Auto-pulls merged code locally after merge
- External contributor PRs logged for manual review
- /api/pr-monitor/status and /api/pr-monitor/trigger endpoints
- Live PR events via SocketIO pr_monitor_event
- Auto-starts when GITHUB_TOKEN is set

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
